links.title: You Told Me to Do It: Measuring Instructional Text-induced Private Data Leakage in LLM Agents

primary_link.title: You Told Me to Do It: Measuring Instructional Text-induced Private Data Leakage in LLM Agents

papers.summary: High-privilege LLM agents that autonomously process external documentation are increasingly trusted to automate tasks by reading and executing project instructions, yet they are granted terminal access, filesystem control, and outbound network connectivity with minimal security oversight. We identify and systematically measure a fundamental vulnerability in this trust model, which we term the \emph{Trusted Executor Dilemma}: agents execute documentation-embedded instructions, including adversari
papers.summary_zh: 能夠自主處理外部文件的具備高權限 LLM agents，越來越被信任用於透過讀取和執行專案指令來自動化任務，然而它們被授予 terminal 存取權、檔案系統控制權和出站網路連線能力，且安全監控極少。我們在這個信任模型中識別並系統性地測量了一個基本漏洞，我們將其稱為「Trusted Executor Dilemma」（信任執行者困境）：agents 會執行文件中嵌入的指令，包括惡意...

links.title: Locating Demographic Bias at the Attention-Head Level in CLIP's Vision Encoder

primary_link.title: Locating Demographic Bias at the Attention-Head Level in CLIP's Vision Encoder

papers.summary: Standard fairness audits of foundation models quantify that a model is biased, but not where inside the network the bias resides. We propose a mechanistic fairness audit that combines projected residual-stream decomposition, zero-shot Concept Activation Vectors, and bias-augmented TextSpan analysis to locate demographic bias at the level of individual attention heads in vision transformers. As a feasibility case study, we apply this pipeline to the CLIP ViT-L-14 encoder on 42 profession classes
papers.summary_zh: 基礎模型的標準公平性審計能夠量化模型是否存在偏見，但無法指出偏見存在於網路的哪個位置。我們提出了一種機械化的公平性審計方法，它結合了 projected residual-stream decomposition、zero-shot Concept Activation Vectors 和 bias-augmented TextSpan analysis，以在 vision transformers 中定位個別 attention head 層級的人口統計學偏見。作為一個可行性案例研究，我們將此流程應用於 42 種職業類別的 CLIP ViT-L-14 encoder。
papers.title: Locating Demographic Bias at the Attention-Head Level in CLIP's Vision Encoder
papers.title_zh: 定位 CLIP Vision Encoder 中 Attention-Head 層級的人口統計學偏見

links.title: Compression Favors Consistency, Not Truth: When and Why Language Models Prefer Correct Information

primary_link.title: Compression Favors Consistency, Not Truth: When and Why Language Models Prefer Correct Information

papers.summary: Why do language models sometimes prefer correct statements even when trained on mixed-quality data? We introduce the Compression--Consistency Principle: next-token prediction favors hypotheses that allow shorter and more internally consistent descriptions of the training data. Truth bias emerges only when false alternatives are structurally harder to compress. We test this using small GPT-2-style character-level transformers (3.5M--86M parameters) on synthetic math corpora with controlled mixtur
papers.summary_zh: 即使在混合品質的數據上進行訓練，語言模型為何有時仍偏愛正確的陳述？我們引入了「Compression--Consistency Principle」（壓縮一致性原則）：下一個 token 的預測偏好能讓訓練數據描述更短且內部更一致的假設。真實性偏見僅在錯誤選項在結構上更難以壓縮時才會出現。我們使用小型 GPT-2-style 字元級 Transformer（3.5M–86M 參數）在具有受控混合... 的合成數學語料庫上測試了這一點。
papers.title: Compression Favors Consistency, Not Truth: When and Why Language Models Prefer Correct Information
papers.title_zh: 壓縮偏愛一致性而非真相：語言模型何時以及為何偏愛正確資訊

links.title: From Control to Foresight: Simulation as a New Paradigm for Human-Agent Collaboration

primary_link.title: From Control to Foresight: Simulation as a New Paradigm for Human-Agent Collaboration

papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used to power autonomous agents for complex, multi-step tasks. However, human-agent interaction remains pointwise and reactive: users approve or correct individual actions to mitigate immediate risks, without visibility into subsequent consequences. This forces users to mentally simulate long-term effects, a cognitively demanding and often inaccurate process. Users have control over individual steps but lack the foresight to make informed decisions.
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 越來越多地被用於驅動自主 agent 來執行複雜的多步驟任務。然而，人機互動仍然是點對點和反應式的：使用者批准或糾正個別行動以減輕即時風險，卻無法預見後續結果。這迫使用戶在心理上模擬長期影響，這是一個認知負擔重且往往不準確的過程。用戶可以控制個別步驟，但缺乏做出明智決策的預見能力。
papers.title: From Control to Foresight: Simulation as a New Paradigm for Human-Agent Collaboration
papers.title_zh: 從控制到預見：模擬作為人機協作的新典範

links.title: OmniStream: Mastering Perception, Reconstruction and Action in Continuous Streams

primary_link.title: OmniStream: Mastering Perception, Reconstruction and Action in Continuous Streams

papers.summary: Modern visual agents require representations that are general, causal, and physically structured to operate in real-time streaming environments. However, current vision foundation models remain fragmented, specializing narrowly in image semantic perception, offline temporal modeling, or spatial geometry. This paper introduces OmniStream, a unified streaming visual backbone that effectively perceives, reconstructs, and acts from diverse visual inputs. By incorporating causal spatiotemporal attention and 3D rotary positional embeddings (3D-RoPE), our model supports efficient, frame-by-frame online processing of video streams via a persistent KV-cache. We pre-train OmniStream using a synergistic multi-task framework coupling static and temporal representation learning, streaming geometric reconstruction, and vision-language alignment on 29 datasets. Extensive evaluations show that, even with a strictly frozen backbone, OmniStream achieves consistently competitive performance with specialized experts across image and video probing, streaming geometric reconstruction, complex video and spatial reasoning, as well as robotic manipulation (unseen at training). Rather than pursuing benchmark-specific dominance, our work demonstrates the viability of training a single, versatile vision backbone that generalizes across semantic, spatial, and temporal reasoning, i.e., a more meaningful step toward general-purpose visual understanding for interactive and embodied agents.
papers.summary_zh: 現代視覺 agent 需要通用、因果且具有物理結構的表示，才能在即時串流環境中運作。然而，目前的 vision foundation models 仍然是碎片化的，僅狹隘地專精於圖像語義感知、離線時間建模或空間幾何。本文介紹了 OmniStream，這是一個統一的串流視覺骨幹網路，能夠有效地從多樣化的視覺輸入中進行感知、重建和行動。透過整合 causal spatiotemporal attention 和 3D rotary positional embeddings (3D-RoPE)，我們的模型透過持久性 KV-cache 支援對視訊串流進行高效的逐幀線上處理。我們使用一個協同的多任務框架預訓練 OmniStream，該框架在 29 個數據集上結合了靜態和時間表徵學習、串流幾何重建以及視覺語言對齊。大量評估表明，即使在骨幹網路嚴格凍結的情況下，OmniStream 在圖像和視訊探測、串流幾何重建、複雜視訊和空間推理以及機器人操作（訓練時未見）方面，都能與專業專家取得始終具有競爭力的表現。我們的研究並非追求特定基準測試的優勢，而是展示了訓練一個單一、多功能視覺骨幹網路的可行性，該網路能夠泛化到語義、空間和時間推理，這代表了邁向互動式和具身 agent 的通用視覺理解更為有意義的一步。
papers.title: OmniStream: Mastering Perception, Reconstruction and Action in Continuous Streams
papers.title_zh: OmniStream：精通連續串流中的感知、重建和行動

links.title: EndoCoT: Scaling Endogenous Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Diffusion Models

primary_link.title: EndoCoT: Scaling Endogenous Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Diffusion Models

papers.summary: Recently, Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have been widely integrated into diffusion frameworks primarily as text encoders to tackle complex tasks such as spatial reasoning. However, this paradigm suffers from two critical limitations: (i) MLLMs text encoder exhibits insufficient reasoning depth. Single-step encoding fails to activate the Chain-of-Thought process, which is essential for MLLMs to provide accurate guidance for complex tasks. (ii) The guidance remains invariant during the decoding process. Invariant guidance during decoding prevents DiT from progressively decomposing complex instructions into actionable denoising steps, even with correct MLLM encodings. To this end, we propose Endogenous Chain-of-Thought (EndoCoT), a novel framework that first activates MLLMs' reasoning potential by iteratively refining latent thought states through an iterative thought guidance module, and then bridges these states to the DiT's denoising process. Second, a terminal thought grounding module is applied to ensure the reasoning trajectory remains grounded in textual supervision by aligning the final state with ground-truth answers. With these two components, the MLLM text encoder delivers meticulously reasoned guidance, enabling the DiT to execute it progressively and ultimately solve complex tasks in a step-by-step manner. Extensive evaluations across diverse benchmarks (e.g., Maze, TSP, VSP, and Sudoku) achieve an average accuracy of 92.1%, outperforming the strongest baseline by 8.3 percentage points.
papers.summary_zh: 近期，Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) 已被廣泛整合到 diffusion frameworks 中，主要作為 text encoders 來處理如空間推理等複雜任務。然而，這種範式存在兩個關鍵限制：(i) MLLMs text encoder 的推理深度不足。單步編碼無法激活 Chain-of-Thought 過程，而這對於 MLLMs 為複雜任務提供精確指導至關重要。(ii) 在解碼過程中，指導保持不變。解碼時不變的指導會阻礙 DiT 即使在 MLLM 編碼正確的情況下，也無法逐步將複雜指令分解為可執行的去噪步驟。為此，我們提出了 Endogenous Chain-of-Thought (EndoCoT)，這是一個新穎的框架，它首先通過迭代思想指導模組(iterative thought guidance module)迭代地精煉潛在思想狀態(latent thought states)，從而激活 MLLMs 的推理潛力，然後將這些狀態橋接到 DiT 的去噪過程。其次，應用一個終端思想奠基模組(terminal thought grounding module)，通過將最終狀態與 ground-truth 答案對齊，確保推理軌跡在文本監督下保持穩固。通過這兩個組件，MLLM text encoder 能夠提供精心推理的指導，使 DiT 能夠逐步執行並最終以循序漸進的方式解決複雜任務。在多個基準測試（例如 Maze, TSP, VSP, 和 Sudoku）上的廣泛評估顯示，平均準確度達到 92.1%，超越最強基線 8.3 個百分點。
papers.title: EndoCoT: Scaling Endogenous Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Diffusion Models
papers.title_zh: EndoCoT: 擴展 Diffusion Models 中的內源性 Chain-of-Thought 推理

links.title: Examining Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges in Non-Verifiable LLM Post-Training

primary_link.title: Examining Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges in Non-Verifiable LLM Post-Training

papers.summary: Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges, which can benefit from inference-time scaling, provide a promising path for extending the success of reasoning models to non-verifiable domains where the output correctness/quality cannot be directly checked. However, while reasoning judges have shown better performance on static evaluation benchmarks, their effectiveness in actual policy training has not been systematically examined. Therefore, we conduct a rigorous study to investigate the actual impact of non-reasoning and reasoning judges in reinforcement-learning-based LLM alignment. Our controlled synthetic setting, where a "gold-standard" judge (gpt-oss-120b) provides preference annotations to train smaller judges, reveals key differences between non-reasoning and reasoning judges: non-reasoning judges lead to reward hacking easily, while reasoning judges can lead to policies that achieve strong performance when evaluated by the gold-standard judge. Interestingly, we find that the reasoning-judge-trained policies achieve such strong performance by learning to generate highly effective adversarial outputs that can also score well on popular benchmarks such as Arena-Hard by deceiving other LLM-judges. Combined with our further analysis, our study highlights both important findings and room for improvements for applying (reasoning) LLM-judges in non-verifiable LLM post-training.
papers.summary_zh: 推理型 LLMs-as-Judges 可以受益於 inference-time scaling，為將推理模型的成功擴展到無法直接檢查輸出正確性/品質的不可驗證領域提供了一條有前景的途徑。然而，儘管推理型 judges 在靜態評估基準測試中表現出更好的性能，但它們在實際 policy training 中的有效性尚未得到系統性檢驗。因此，我們進行了一項嚴格的研究，以調查非推理型和推理型 judges 在基於 reinforcement-learning 的 LLM alignment 中的實際影響。我們在受控的合成設定中，由一個「gold-standard」 judge (gpt-oss-120b) 提供偏好註釋以訓練較小的 judges，揭示了非推理型和推理型 judges 之間的關鍵差異：非推理型 judges 容易導致 reward hacking，而推理型 judges 可以使 policy 在由 gold-standard judge 評估時達到強勁的性能。有趣的是，我們發現推理型 judge 訓練出的 policy 能夠通過學習生成高效的對抗性輸出，進而欺騙其他 LLM-judges，使其在 Arena-Hard 等流行基準測試中也能獲得高分，從而實現如此強勁的性能。結合我們的進一步分析，本研究強調了在不可驗證的 LLM post-training 中應用（推理型）LLM-judges 的重要發現和改進空間。
papers.title: Examining Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges in Non-Verifiable LLM Post-Training
papers.title_zh: 檢視推理型 LLMs-as-Judges 在不可驗證的 LLM 後訓練中的應用

links.title: EmbTracker: Traceable Black-box Watermarking for Federated Language Models

primary_link.title: EmbTracker: Traceable Black-box Watermarking for Federated Language Models

papers.summary: Federated Language Model (FedLM) allows a collaborative learning without sharing raw data, yet it introduces a critical vulnerability, as every untrustworthy client may leak the received functional model instance. Current watermarking schemes for FedLM often require white-box access and client-side cooperation, providing only group-level proof of ownership rather than individual traceability. We propose EmbTracker, a server-side, traceable black-box watermarking framework specifically designed for FedLMs. EmbTracker achieves black-box verifiability by embedding a backdoor-based watermark detectable through simple API queries. Client-level traceability is realized by injecting unique identity-specific watermarks into the model distributed to each client. In this way, a leaked model can be attributed to a specific culprit, ensuring robustness even against non-cooperative participants. Extensive experiments on various language and vision-language models demonstrate that EmbTracker achieves robust traceability with verification rates near 100\%, high resilience against removal attacks (fine-tuning, pruning, quantization), and negligible impact on primary task performance (typically within 1-2\%).
papers.summary_zh: 聯邦式語言模型 (FedLM) 允許在不共享原始數據的情況下進行協作學習，但它引入了一個關鍵的漏洞，因為每個不可信的客戶端都可能洩露其接收到的功能模型實例。目前針對 FedLM 的浮水印方案通常需要 white-box 訪問和客戶端協作，僅提供群體層級的所有權證明，而非個別可追溯性。我們提出了 EmbTracker，這是一個專為 FedLM 設計的伺服器端、可追溯的 black-box 浮水印框架。EmbTracker 通過嵌入一個基於後門的浮水印來實現 black-box 可驗證性，該浮水印可以通過簡單的 API 查詢檢測到。客戶端層級的可追溯性是通過向分發給每個客戶端的模型注入獨特的身份專屬浮水印來實現的。通過這種方式，洩露的模型可以歸因於特定的肇事者，即使面對不合作的參與者也能確保其穩健性。對各種語言和視覺-語言模型進行的廣泛實驗證明，EmbTracker 實現了穩健的可追溯性，驗證率接近 100%，對移除攻擊（fine-tuning, pruning, quantization）具有高彈性，並且對主要任務性能的影響可忽略不計（通常在 1-2% 以內）。
papers.title: EmbTracker: Traceable Black-box Watermarking for Federated Language Models
papers.title_zh: EmbTracker：針對聯邦式語言模型的黑箱可追溯浮水印

links.title: Slow-Fast Inference: Training-Free Inference Acceleration via Within-Sentence Support Stability

primary_link.title: Slow-Fast Inference: Training-Free Inference Acceleration via Within-Sentence Support Stability

papers.summary: Long-context autoregressive decoding remains expensive because each decoding step must repeatedly process a growing history. We observe a consistent pattern during decoding: within a sentence, and more generally within a short semantically coherent span, the dominant attention support often remains largely stable. Motivated by this observation, we propose Slow-Fast Inference (SFI), a training-free decoding framework that decouples generation into frequent low-cost fast steps and occasional dense
papers.summary_zh: 長上下文的 autoregressive decoding 仍然成本高昂，因為每個解碼步驟都必須重複處理不斷增長的歷史記錄。我們在解碼過程中觀察到一個一致的模式：在一個句子內，更廣泛地說，在一個短的語義連貫範圍內，主導的 attention support 常常保持大致穩定。受此觀察的啟發，我們提出了 Slow-Fast Inference (SFI)，這是一個免訓練的解碼框架，它將生成解耦為頻繁的低成本快速步驟和偶爾的密集

primary_link.title: Scaling Laws for Educational AI Agents

We propose that educational agent capability scales not merely with the underlying model size, but through structured dimensions that we collectively term the Agent Scaling Law: role definition clarity, skill depth, tool completeness, runtime capability, and educator expertise i
papers.summary_zh: 儘管 Large Language Models (LLMs) 的 scaling laws 已在模型參數、訓練數據和算力等維度上得到廣泛研究，但基於 LLM 的 educational agents 的 scaling behavior 仍未被探索。我們提出 educational agent 的能力不僅隨著底層模型規模擴展，而是透過我們統稱為 Agent Scaling Law 的結構化維度來擴展：role definition clarity、skill depth、tool completeness、runtime capability 和 educator expertise...

links.title: Entropy-Preserving Reinforcement Learning

primary_link.title: Entropy-Preserving Reinforcement Learning

papers.summary: Policy gradient algorithms have driven many recent advancements in language model reasoning. An appealing property is their ability to learn from exploration on their own trajectories, a process crucial for fostering diverse and creative solutions. links.title: GRADE: Benchmarking Discipline-Informed Reasoning in Image Editing

primary_link.title: GRADE: Benchmarking Discipline-Informed Reasoning in Image Editing

papers.summary: Unified multimodal models target joint understanding, reasoning, and generation, but current image editing benchmarks are largely confined to natural images and shallow commonsense reasoning, offering limited assessment of this capability under structured, domain-specific constraints. In this work, we introduce GRADE, the first benchmark to assess discipline-informed knowledge and reasoning in image editing. GRADE comprises 520 carefully curated samples across 10 academic domains, spanning from natural science to social science. To support rigorous evaluation, we propose a multi-dimensional evaluation protocol that jointly assesses Discipline Reasoning, Visual Consistency, and Logical Readability. Extensive experiments on 20 state-of-the-art open-source and closed-source models reveal substantial limitations in current models under implicit, knowledge-intensive editing settings, leading to large performance gaps. Beyond quantitative scores, we conduct rigorous analyses and ablations to expose model shortcomings and identify the constraints within disciplinary editing. Together, GRADE pinpoints key directions for the future development of unified multimodal models, advancing the research on discipline-informed image editing and reasoning. Our benchmark and evaluation code are publicly released.

links.title: WeEdit: A Dataset, Benchmark and Glyph-Guided Framework for Text-centric Image Editing

primary_link.title: WeEdit: A Dataset, Benchmark and Glyph-Guided Framework for Text-centric Image Editing

papers.summary: Instruction-based image editing aims to modify specific content within existing images according to user-provided instructions while preserving non-target regions. Beyond traditional object- and style-centric manipulation, text-centric image editing focuses on modifying, translating, or rearranging textual elements embedded within images. However, existing leading models often struggle to execute complex text editing precisely, frequently producing blurry or hallucinated characters. We attribute these failures primarily to the lack of specialized training paradigms tailored for text-centric editing, as well as the absence of large-scale datasets and standardized benchmarks necessary for a closed-loop training and evaluation system. To address these limitations, we present WeEdit, a systematic solution encompassing a scalable data construction pipeline, two benchmarks, and a tailored two-stage training strategy. Specifically, we propose a novel HTML-based automatic editing pipeline, which generates 330K training pairs covering diverse editing operations and 15 languages, accompanied by standardized bilingual and multilingual benchmarks for comprehensive evaluation. On the algorithmic side, we employ glyph-guided supervised fine-tuning to inject explicit spatial and content priors, followed by a multi-objective reinforcement learning stage to align generation with instruction adherence, text clarity, and background preservation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that WeEdit outperforms previous open-source models by a clear margin across diverse editing operations.
papers.summary_zh: 基於指令的圖像編輯旨在根據用戶提供的指令修改現有圖像中的特定內容，同時保留非目標區域。除了傳統的以物件和風格為中心的操縱之外，以文本為中心的圖像編輯專注於修改、翻譯或重新排列圖像中嵌入的文本元素。然而，現有的領先模型在精確執行複雜文本編輯時常遇到困難，經常產生模糊或幻覺字符。我們將這些失敗主要歸因於缺乏針對以文本為中心編輯的專業訓練範式，以及缺乏建立閉環訓練和評估系統所需的大規模資料集和標準化基準。為了應對這些限制，我們提出了 WeEdit，這是一個系統性解決方案，包含一個可擴展的資料建構流程、兩個基準，以及一個專門的兩階段訓練策略。具體而言，我們提出了一種新穎的基於 HTML 的自動編輯流程，該流程生成了 330K 個訓練對，涵蓋了多樣的編輯操作和 15 種語言，並附有標準化的雙語和多語基準，以進行全面的評估。在演算法方面，我們採用了 glyph-guided supervised fine-tuning 來注入明確的空間和內容先驗知識，隨後是一個 multi-objective reinforcement learning 階段，以使生成結果與指令遵循、文本清晰度和背景保留保持一致。大量實驗證明，WeEdit 在多樣的編輯操作中，明顯優於先前的開源模型。

links.title: SemBench: A Universal Semantic Framework for LLM Evaluation

primary_link.title: SemBench: A Universal Semantic Framework for LLM Evaluation

radar.summary: Recent progress in Natural Language Processing (NLP) has been driven by the emergence of Large Language Models (LLMs), which exhibit remarkable generative and reasoning capabilities. However, despite their success, evaluating the true semantic understanding of these models remains a persistent challenge. Traditional benchmarks such as Word-in-Context (WiC) effectively probe this capability, but their creation is resource-intensive and often limited to high-resource languages. In this paper, we i
radar.summary_zh: 近期 Natural Language Processing (NLP) 的進展，主要歸因於 Large Language Models (LLMs) 的興起，這些模型展現出卓越的生成和推理能力。然而，儘管取得了成功，評估這些模型的真實 semantic understanding 仍然是一個持續的挑戰。傳統的 benchmarks，例如 Word-in-Context (WiC)，能有效探測此能力，但它們的創建需要大量資源，且通常僅限於 high-resource languages。在本文中，我們探討了此議題。

links.title: See, Symbolize, Act: Grounding VLMs with Spatial Representations for Better Gameplay

primary_link.title: See, Symbolize, Act: Grounding VLMs with Spatial Representations for Better Gameplay

radar.summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) excel at describing visual scenes, yet struggle to translate perception into precise, grounded actions. We investigate whether providing VLMs with both the visual frame and the symbolic representation of the scene can improve their performance in interactive environments. We evaluate three state-of-the-art VLMs across Atari games, VizDoom, and AI2-THOR, comparing frame-only, frame with self-extracted symbols, frame with ground-truth symbols, and symbol-only pipeline
radar.summary_zh: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) 在描述視覺場景方面表現出色，但卻難以將感知轉化為精確且 grounded 的行動。我們研究是否能透過向 VLMs 提供視覺 frame 和場景的 symbolic representation 來提高它們在 interactive environments 中的表現。我們評估了三種 state-of-the-art VLMs，涵蓋 Atari games、VizDoom 和 AI2-THOR，比較了僅有 frame、帶有 self-extracted symbols 的 frame、帶有 ground-truth symbols 的 frame，以及僅有 symbol 的 pipeline。

primary_link.title: OMNIA: Closing the Loop by Leveraging LLMs for Knowledge Graph Completion

radar.summary: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) are widely used to represent structured knowledge, yet their automatic construction, especially with Large Language Models (LLMs), often results in incomplete or noisy outputs. Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC) aims to infer and add missing triples, but most existing methods either rely on structural embeddings that overlook semantics or language models that ignore the graph's structure and depend on external sources. In this work, we present OMNIA, a two-stage approach tha
radar.summary_zh: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) 被廣泛用於表示 structured knowledge，然而其自動構建，特別是使用 Large Language Models (LLMs) 時，往往會產生不完整或嘈雜的輸出。Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC) 旨在推斷並添加缺失的 triples，但大多數現有方法要麼依賴於忽略 semantics 的 structural embeddings，要麼依賴於忽略圖結構並依賴外部來源的 language models。在這項工作中，我們提出了 OMNIA，這是一種兩階段方法，旨在解決這些限制。
radar.title: OMNIA: Closing the Loop by Leveraging LLMs for Knowledge Graph Completion

links.title: Video Streaming Thinking: VideoLLMs Can Watch and Think Simultaneously

radar.summary: Online Video Large Language Models (VideoLLMs) play a critical role in supporting responsive, real-time interaction. Existing methods focus on streaming perception, lacking a synchronized logical reasoning stream. However, directly applying test-time scaling methods incurs unacceptable response latency. To address this trade-off, we propose Video Streaming Thinking (VST), a novel paradigm for streaming video understanding. It supports a thinking while watching mechanism, which activates reasonin
radar.summary_zh: Online Video Large Language Models (VideoLLMs) 在支援響應式、real-time interaction 方面扮演著關鍵角色。現有方法側重於 streaming perception，卻缺乏同步的 logical reasoning stream。然而，直接應用 test-time scaling methods 會導致不可接受的 response latency。為了解決這種 trade-off，我們提出了 Video Streaming Thinking (VST)，這是一種用於 streaming video understanding 的新型範式。它支援一種 thinking while watching 機制，該機制啟動了推理流程。
radar.title: Video Streaming Thinking: VideoLLMs Can Watch and Think Simultaneously
radar.title_zh: 影片串流思考：VideoLLMs 可以同時觀看與思考

links.title: IndexCache: Accelerating Sparse Attention via Cross-Layer Index Reuse

primary_link.title: IndexCache: Accelerating Sparse Attention via Cross-Layer Index Reuse

top5.summary: Long-context agentic workflows have emerged as a defining use case for large language models, making attention efficiency critical for both inference speed and serving cost. Sparse attention addresses this challenge effectively, and DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) is a representative production-grade solution: a lightweight lightning indexer selects the top-k most relevant tokens per query, reducing core attention from O(L^2) to O(Lk). However, the indexer itself retains O(L^2) complexity and must run independently at every layer, despite the fact that the resulting top-k selections are highly similar across consecutive layers. We present IndexCache, which exploits this cross-layer redundancy by partitioning layers into a small set of Full layers that run their own indexers and a majority of Shared layers that simply reuse the nearest Full layer's top-k indices. We propose two complementary approaches to determine and optimize this configuration. Training-free IndexCache applies a greedy search algorithm that selects which layers to retain indexers by directly minimizing language modeling loss on a calibration set, requiring no weight updates. Training-aware IndexCache introduces a multi-layer distillation loss that trains each retained indexer against the averaged attention distributions of all layers it serves, enabling even simple interleaved patterns to match full-indexer accuracy. Experimental results on a 30B DSA model show that IndexCache can remove 75% of indexer computations with negligible quality degradation, achieving up to 1.82times prefill speedup and 1.48times decode speedup compared to standard DSA. These positive results are further confirmed by our preliminary experiments on the production-scale GLM-5 model (Figure 1).
top5.summary_zh: 長上下文的 agentic 工作流程已成為大型語言模型的一個關鍵應用案例，使得注意力效率對於推理速度和服務成本都至關重要。Sparse attention 有效地解決了這個挑戰，而 DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) 則是一個具代表性的生產級解決方案：一個輕量級的快速索引器為每個 query 選擇前 k 個最相關的 token，將核心注意力的複雜度從 O(L^2) 降低到 O(Lk)。然而，索引器本身仍保持 O(L^2) 的複雜度，並且必須在每個層獨立運行，儘管在連續層之間所產生的前 k 個選擇高度相似。我們提出了 IndexCache，它透過將層劃分為一小部分運行自己索引器的 Full layers 和大多數僅重用最近 Full layer 的前 k 個索引的 Shared layers 來利用這種跨層冗餘。我們提出了兩種互補的方法來確定和優化此配置。Training-free IndexCache 採用貪婪搜索演算法，透過直接最小化校準集上的語言模型損失來選擇保留哪些層的索引器，無需權重更新。Training-aware IndexCache 引入了一種多層蒸餾損失，該損失針對其所服務的所有層的平均注意力分佈來訓練每個保留的索引器，即使是簡單的交錯模式也能達到全索引器的準確性。在 30B DSA 模型上的實驗結果表明，IndexCache 可以消除 75% 的索引器計算，且品質退化可忽略不計，與標準 DSA 相比，實現了高達 1.82 倍的預填充加速和 1.48 倍的解碼加速。這些積極的結果在我們對生產規模的 GLM-5 模型（圖 1）的初步實驗中得到了進一步證實。
top5.title: IndexCache: Accelerating Sparse Attention via Cross-Layer Index Reuse
top5.title_zh: IndexCache：透過跨層索引重用加速 Sparse Attention

primary_link.title: Taming OpenClaw: Security Analysis and Mitigation of Autonomous LLM Agent Threats

top5.summary: Autonomous Large Language Model (LLM) agents, exemplified by OpenClaw, demonstrate remarkable capabilities in executing complex, long-horizon tasks. However, their tightly coupled instant-messaging interaction paradigm and high-privilege execution capabilities substantially expand the system attack surface. In this paper, we present a comprehensive security threat analysis of OpenClaw. To structure our analysis, we introduce a five-layer lifecycle-oriented security framework that captures key st
top5.summary_zh: 自主大型語言模型 (LLM) agent，以 OpenClaw 為例，展現了在執行複雜、長期任務方面的卓越能力。然而，它們緊密耦合的即時通訊互動範式和高權限執行能力，大幅擴大了系統的攻擊面。在本文中，我們對 OpenClaw 進行了全面的安全威脅分析。為了建構我們的分析，我們引入了一個五層生命週期導向的安全框架，該框架捕捉了關鍵的安全性特點。

links.title: Automatic Generation of High-Performance RL Environments

primary_link.title: Automatic Generation of High-Performance RL Environments

top5.summary: Translating complex reinforcement learning (RL) environments into high-performance implementations has traditionally required months of specialized engineering. We present a reusable recipe - a generic prompt template, hierarchical verification, and iterative agent-assisted repair - that produces semantically equivalent high-performance environments for <$10 in compute cost. We demonstrate three distinct workflows across five environments. Direct translation (no prior performance implementation exists): EmuRust (1.5x PPO speedup via Rust parallelism for a Game Boy emulator) and PokeJAX, the first GPU-parallel Pokemon battle simulator (500M SPS random action, 15.2M SPS PPO; 22,320x over the TypeScript reference). Translation verified against existing performance implementations: throughput parity with MJX (1.04x) and 5x over Brax at matched GPU batch sizes (HalfCheetah JAX); 42x PPO (Puffer Pong). New environment creation: TCGJax, the first deployable JAX Pokemon TCG engine (717K SPS random action, 153K SPS PPO; 6.6x over the Python reference), synthesized from a web-extracted specification. At 200M parameters, the environment overhead drops below 4% of training time. Hierarchical verification (property, interaction, and rollout tests) confirms semantic equivalence for all five environments; cross-backend policy transfer confirms zero sim-to-sim gap for all five environments. TCGJax, synthesized from a private reference absent from public repositories, serves as a contamination control for agent pretraining data concerns. The paper contains sufficient detail - including representative prompts, verification methodology, and complete results - that a coding agent could reproduce the translations directly from the manuscript.
top5.summary_zh: 將複雜的強化學習 (RL) 環境轉化為高效能實作，傳統上需要數月的專業工程。我們提出了一個可重複使用的配方——一個通用的 prompt 模板、分層驗證和迭代式 agent 輔助修復——它能以低於 10 美元的計算成本，產生語義等效的高效能環境。我們在五個環境中展示了三種不同的工作流程。直接翻譯（不存在先前的效能實作）：EmuRust（透過 Rust 並行化實現 Game Boy 模擬器 1.5 倍 PPO 加速）和 PokeJAX，第一個 GPU 並行 Pokemon 對戰模擬器（5 億 SPS 隨機動作，15.2 萬 SPS PPO；比 TypeScript 參考快 22,320 倍）。針對現有高效能實作進行驗證的翻譯：與 MJX 達到吞吐量一致（1.04 倍），在匹配的 GPU 批次大小下比 Brax 快 5 倍（HalfCheetah JAX）；42 倍 PPO（Puffer Pong）。新環境創建：TCGJax，第一個可部署的 JAX Pokemon TCG 引擎（71.7 萬 SPS 隨機動作，15.3 萬 SPS PPO；比 Python 參考快 6.6 倍），從網路提取的規範合成。在 2 億參數時，環境開銷降至訓練時間的 4% 以下。分層驗證（屬性、互動和 rollout 測試）證實了所有五個環境的語義等效性；跨後端策略遷移證實了所有五個環境的零 sim-to-sim 差距。TCGJax 是從公共儲存庫中沒有的私有參考合成的，作為 agent 預訓練資料問題的污染控制。本文包含足夠的細節——包括代表性 prompt、驗證方法論和完整結果——以便編碼 agent 可以直接從手稿重現這些翻譯。
top5.title: Automatic Generation of High-Performance RL Environments
top5.title_zh: 高效能 RL 環境的自動生成

links.title: XSkill: Continual Learning from Experience and Skills in Multimodal Agents

primary_link.title: XSkill: Continual Learning from Experience and Skills in Multimodal Agents

top5.summary: Multimodal agents can now tackle complex reasoning tasks with diverse tools, yet they still suffer from inefficient tool use and inflexible orchestration in open-ended settings. top5.summary_zh: 多模態 agent 現在可以利用多樣化工具處理複雜的推理任務，但它們在開放式環境中仍然面臨工具使用效率低下和編排不靈活的問題。一個核心挑戰是如何讓這些 agent 透過從過去的軌跡中學習，在不更新參數的情況下持續改進。我們識別出兩種對此目標至關重要的互補可重用知識形式：經驗 (experiences)，提供用於工具選擇和決策的簡潔行動層級指導；以及技能 (skills)，提供

links.title: CLASP: Defending Hybrid Large Language Models Against Hidden State Poisoning Attacks

primary_link.title: CLASP: Defending Hybrid Large Language Models Against Hidden State Poisoning Attacks

top5.summary: State space models (SSMs) like Mamba have gained significant traction as efficient alternatives to Transformers, achieving linear complexity while maintaining competitive performance. However, Hidden State Poisoning Attacks (HiSPAs), a recently discovered vulnerability that corrupts SSM memory through adversarial strings, pose a critical threat to these architectures and their hybrid variants. Framing the HiSPA mitigation task as a binary classification problem at the token level, we introduce t
top5.summary_zh: 狀態空間模型 (SSMs) 如 Mamba 已作為 Transformer 的高效替代方案獲得了顯著關注，它們實現了線性複雜度並保持了競爭性效能。然而，隱藏狀態中毒攻擊 (HiSPAs) 是一種最近發現的漏洞，它透過對抗性字串損壞 SSM 記憶體，對這些架構及其混合變體構成了關鍵威脅。本文將 HiSPA 緩解任務視為 token 層級的二元分類問題，我們引入了 CLASP。
top5.title: CLASP: Defending Hybrid Large Language Models Against Hidden State Poisoning Attacks
top5.title_zh: CLASP：防禦混合型大型語言模型免受隱藏狀態中毒攻擊
